Genes.Causal_Chain: SPL9 protein directly binds to PAP1 (MYB) via R2R3 domain, competing with TT8 (bHLH) for PAP1 binding, thereby destabilizing the MYB-bHLH-WD40 transcriptional activation complex. This represses expression of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes (DFR, ANS, F3'H, UGT75C1), redirecting dihydroflavonol flux from anthocyanin toward flavonol production.
Genes.Effect_Direction: SPL9 negatively regulates anthocyanin accumulation and positively affects flavonol accumulation
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation + metabolite quantification; ChIP showing SPL9 binds DFR promoter (Figure 5D); DEX+CHX inducible system showing direct transcriptional repression (Figure 5B); Yeast two-hybrid, BiFC, and CoIP confirming SPL9-PAP1 protein interaction (Figure 6A-D); Competition assay showing SPL9 disrupts PAP1-TT8 interaction (Figure 6E)
Genes.Genotype_Lines: Pro35S:MIR156, Pro35S:MIM156, Pro35S:rSPL9, ProSPL9:rSPL9, ProSPL9:rSPL9-GR, ProSPL9:GFP-rSPL9, spl9-4 spl15-1
Genes.Intermediate_Metabolites_Affected: Dihydrokaempferol flux toward DFR (anthocyanin) vs FLS (flavonol) branch
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Overexpression (Pro35S:rSPL9, ProSPL9:rSPL9), miR156 overexpression (Pro35S:MIR156, reduces SPL levels), miR156 target mimicry (Pro35S:MIM156, increases SPL levels), inducible activation (ProSPL9:rSPL9-GR + DEX), loss-of-function (spl9-4 spl15-1 double mutant)
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Pro35S:MIR156 (low SPL): ~4-fold increase in anthocyanin, flavonols reduced to <1/10 of wild type; Pro35S:MIM156 (high SPL): anthocyanin reduced to ~1/10, flavonols increased 1.6- to 2.9-fold; DEX-induced rSPL9-GR: ~60% decrease in DFR transcripts after 6h DEX+CHX treatment

Genes.Causal_Chain: miR156 targets and degrades SPL mRNAs. High miR156 → low SPL protein → intact MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex → high expression of DFR, ANS, F3'H, UGT75C1 → high anthocyanin, low flavonol. Low miR156 (MIM156) → high SPL → disrupted MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex → low anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression → low anthocyanin, high flavonol.
Genes.Effect_Direction: Increased miR156 promotes anthocyanin accumulation and decreases flavonol levels; decreased miR156 activity has opposite effects
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation (overexpression and target mimicry) + metabolite quantification (anthocyanin at A530, LC-MS flavonol) + gene expression (qRT-PCR)
Genes.Gene_Role_Category: Regulatory TF/signaling gene (miRNA regulator of SPLs)
Genes.Intermediate_Metabolites_Affected: Dihydrokaempferol partitioning between anthocyanin and flavonol branches
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthetic pathway (anthocyanin branch) via post-transcriptional regulation of SPL transcription factors
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Pro35S:MIR156: ~4-fold increase in anthocyanin in stems (Figure 1F); flavonols reduced to <1/10 of wild type (Figure 2D); DFR transcripts increased >30-fold (Figure 3A). Pro35S:MIM156: anthocyanin reduced to ~1/10 of wild type; flavonols increased 1.6- to 2.9-fold

Genes.Causal_Chain: DFR is a direct transcriptional target of SPL9 (ChIP-confirmed, Figure 5D). SPL9 is recruited to DFR promoter indirectly (not via GTAC boxes, Figure 5E) through interaction with the MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex. DFR catalyzes the first committed step toward anthocyanin biosynthesis from dihydroflavonols.
Genes.Effect_Direction: DFR expression is positively correlated with anthocyanin accumulation; repressed by SPL9
Genes.Evidence_Type: ChIP (Figure 5D); DEX+CHX inducible expression assay (Figure 5B); qRT-PCR (Figure 3A); GUS reporter (Figure 4B); GTAC mutation reporter (Figure 5E)
Genes.Genotype_Lines: ProDFR:GUS, ProDFR:GUS mu (GTAC mutated), Pro35S:MIR156, Pro35S:MIM156, ProSPL9:rSPL9-GR, ProSPL9:GFP-rSPL9
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthetic pathway — first committed step of anthocyanin branch (dihydroflavonol → leucoanthocyanidin)
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Transcriptional regulation monitored via ProDFR:GUS reporter, qRT-PCR expression analysis in Pro35S:MIR156 and Pro35S:MIM156 backgrounds; direct target validation by ChIP and DEX-inducible SPL9 system
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: DFR transcripts >30-fold increased in Pro35S:MIR156 vs wild type; decreased in Pro35S:MIM156; ~60% decrease after 6h DEX+CHX treatment in ProSPL9:rSPL9-GR (Figure 5B); DFR expression highest at stem base and lowest at apex, inversely correlated with SPL9 expression (Figure 4A)

Genes.Causal_Chain: PAP1 (MYB) forms MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with TT8 and TTG1 to activate anthocyanin biosynthetic genes. SPL9 competes with TT8 for binding to PAP1's R2R3 domain (Figure 6A-B), destabilizing the complex. Loss of PAP1 abolishes the anthocyanin-promoting effect of high miR156.
Genes.Effect_Direction: PAP1 positively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis; loss of PAP1 suppresses anthocyanin hyperaccumulation in Pro35S:MIR156 background
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation (pap1-migs knockdown) + phenotype; Yeast two-hybrid (Figure 6A-B); BiFC (Figure 6C); CoIP (Figure 6D); Competition assay (Figure 6E)
Genes.Gene_Role_Category: Regulatory TF/signaling gene (MYB component of MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex)
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthetic pathway — transcriptional activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Knockdown (pap1-migs using miRNA-induced gene silencing); protein-protein interaction assays (yeast two-hybrid, BiFC, CoIP)
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: pap1-migs: much reduced anthocyanin at hypocotyl-cotyledon and rosette-stem junctions; pap1-migs sufficient to suppress anthocyanin accumulation in Pro35S:MIR156 inflorescences (Supplemental Figure 4)

Genes.Causal_Chain: TTG1 (WD40) is an essential component of the MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex that activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes. Loss of TTG1 abolishes the anthocyanin-promoting effect of miR156 overexpression.
Genes.Effect_Direction: TTG1 is required for anthocyanin accumulation; ttg1-1 suppresses anthocyanin hyperaccumulation in Pro35S:MIR156
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation (ttg1-1 mutant) + phenotype in Pro35S:MIR156 background
Genes.Gene_Role_Category: Regulatory TF/signaling gene (WD40 component of MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex)
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthetic pathway — transcriptional activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: ttg1-1 sufficient to suppress anthocyanin accumulation in Pro35S:MIR156 inflorescences (Supplemental Figure 4)

Genes.Causal_Chain: TT8 (bHLH) binds PAP1 (MYB) R2R3 domain to form the MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex. SPL9 competes with TT8 for binding to PAP1's R2R3 domain, disrupting complex formation and suppressing anthocyanin gene expression.
Genes.Effect_Direction: TT8 positively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis; tt8-1 partially reduces anthocyanin in Pro35S:MIR156 (due to redundancy with GL3/EGL3); SPL9 competes with TT8 for PAP1 binding
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation (tt8-1 mutant in Pro35S:MIR156 background); Yeast two-hybrid (Figure 6A-B); BiFC competition assay (Figure 6E)
Genes.Gene_Role_Category: Regulatory TF/signaling gene (bHLH component of MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex)
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthetic pathway — transcriptional activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Loss-of-function mutant (tt8-1); protein-protein interaction assays (yeast two-hybrid, BiFC competition assay)
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: tt8-1 only reduced (not abolished) anthocyanin accumulation in Pro35S:MIR156 inflorescences (Supplemental Figure 4); SPL9 co-expression greatly suppressed PAP1-TT8 BiFC signal (Figure 6E)

Genes.Causal_Chain: SPL10 (SPL9 subfamily member) overexpression phenocopies SPL9 overexpression in reducing anthocyanin, consistent with redundant negative regulation of anthocyanin biosynthesis by SPL9-type SPLs.
Genes.Effect_Direction: Overexpression of SPL10 decreases anthocyanin accumulation at stem-rosette junction
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Decreased anthocyanin accumulation at stem-rosette junction (Supplemental Figure 2)

Genes.Causal_Chain: SPL3 subfamily plays only a minor role, if any, in regulating anthocyanin biosynthesis. SPL3/SPL4 expression did not change along the stem (Figure 4A), consistent with lack of impact on anthocyanin.
Genes.Effect_Direction: No significant effect on anthocyanin accumulation — neither loss-of-function nor overexpression altered anthocyanin levels
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation (loss-of-function and overexpression) + phenotype observation
Genes.Gene_Role_Category: Regulatory TF/signaling gene (negative result — minor or no role in anthocyanin regulation)
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: spl3-1 had same purple pigments as wild-type Ws; overexpression of SPL3 did not decrease anthocyanin (Supplemental Figure 2)
Genes.Tissue_Stage: Seedlings, inflorescences (Ws ecotype for spl3-1; Col-0 for overexpression)

Title: Negative Regulation of Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in Arabidopsis by a miR156-Targeted SPL Transcription Factor
